outputs/scale_test_results.md.text: # Madison SyntheticPersonas — Scale Testing Results ## Scale Testing Results - Single request (1 persona): 17.8 seconds - 5 requests: 19.0 seconds total - 10 requests: 30.4 seconds total - 15 requests (full dataset): 23.9 seconds total - 25 requests: 21.5 seconds total (9 failures) - 50 requests: 21.3 seconds total (35 failures) - Maximum capacity: ~15 requests before rate limit failures begin ## What Breaks First - API rate limits hit at: ~16 requests (HTTP 429 from Claude API) - Memory issues at: Not observed within test range - Timeout errors at: Not observed — all failures were rate limit errors - Cost per 100 requests: ~$0.10 (Claude API at $0.001 per persona) ## Production Readiness - Could this run 24/7? YES — with request spacing of 2+ seconds between calls - Estimated monthly cost at 1000 requests/day: ~$30/month (Claude API only) - Required monitoring: - Claude API error rate (alert at >10% failures) - Execution time per run (alert at >60 seconds) - Email delivery confirmation - Cost per day (alert at >$1.00) ## Error Handling at Scale - All 35 rate limit errors at 50 requests were caught by Error Handler node - Fallback persona generated for every failed record - Workflow never crashed — always completed with partial results - Error type: AxiosError HTTP 429 — "Try spacing your requests out" ## Breaking Point Evidence - 1-15 records: 100% success rate - 25 records: 64% success rate (9/25 failed) - 50 records: 30% success rate (15/50 succeeded) - Root cause: Claude API free tier rate limit (~15 requests/minute) - Fix for production: Add 2-second delay between requests OR upgrade API tier ## Cost Analysis | Volume | Cost per Run | Monthly (daily) | |--------|-------------|-----------------| | 15 personas | $0.016 | $0.48/month | | 50 personas | $0.051 | $1.53/month | | 1000 personas/day | $1.00 | $30/month | ## Limitations of This Test - Memory usage was not measured — n8n UI does not expose RAM metrics. To measure in production: run `top` command during execution. - Timeout errors were not triggered — all failures at 25+ requests were rate limit errors (HTTP 429) that occurred before timeout threshold. - Tests run sequentially on local machine — production server performance may differ significantly.
